name: pool-route
description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router + SessionStart routing-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make · cargo · npm · gradle · pytest · go build · swift build · docker build · nvidia-smi · train · hexa kick/drill/loop/cc · `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan · …) or a root-needing command, the command is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh (`ssh <host> 'cd <workdir> && <cmd>'`) — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + `hexa <subverb> --help` dispatchable for kick/drill/loop/cc), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown so the operator fixes the pool host setup — never silently falls back to local execution. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang` (absorbed verbs need the repo); other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100 entries); the SessionStart event reads the last 5 entries back and emits them as additionalContext. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO opt-out by design.
